signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives inflation surge, pushing Fed rate cuts to late 2026 — Reuters poll. This fuels safe-haven demand, but USD/JPY at 159.48 is supported by a 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential. BoJ intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00; 159.00 is the first technical level on a risk-off move.
evidence: Reuters poll: Fed rate cuts pushed to late 2026

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) vs retail 89% short (extreme retail short). HV30 at 6.8%, ATM IV at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.4 vol-pt premium over realized. Late-trend caution; retail capitulation risk if price extends higher. Watch for LF delta turning positive or AM reducing to shift the read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp EUR yield disadvantage. EUR/USD at 1.1708, testing the 1.1720 range top. Positioning neutral (COT LF net short -72); AM constructive. A break above 1.1720 could signal a short-term uptrend.

signals.title: GBP/JPY — Carry + Technical
signals.text: GBP/JPY at 215.235, supported by a 300bp BoE-BoJ carry premium. The pair is near the upper end of its recent range; a sustained break above 216.00 could signal further upside. Watch for UK PMI data to confirm the BoE's hawkish stance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Positioning + Retail
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -17,090 (bearish spec positioning) vs retail 63% short. NZD/USD at 0.5900, near the lower end of its recent range. A break below 0.5850 could signal further downside.
